code_generation_patterns.overview: Concrete code templates and FastAPI patterns for generating the multi-agent system backend. Stack: FastAPI + LangGraph + ChatOllama (langchain-ollama). Use these templates as the starting point for each generated file. Customise to match implementation.md exactly — don't copy templates verbatim.

fastapi_app_template.description: Main FastAPI application entry point. Graph is built once in lifespan and stored on app.state.
fastapi_app_template.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
fastapi_app_template.template:
# backend/app/main.py import logging import sys from contextlib import asynccontextmanager from fastapi import FastAPI from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse import httpx
from app.config import settings from app.api.v1.routes import router as v1_router from app.graph.builder import build_graph
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
def setup_logging() -> None: logging.basicConfig( level=getattr(logging, settings.log_level.upper(), logging.INFO), format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s", handlers=[logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)], )
@asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(app: FastAPI): setup_logging()
# Check Ollama connectivity async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client: try: r = await client.get(settings.ollama_base_url) if r.status_code == 200: logger.info("Ollama connected at %s", settings.ollama_base_url) else: logger.warning("Ollama responded %d — agents may fail", r.status_code) except Exception: logger.warning( "Ollama unreachable at %s — start with 'ollama serve'", settings.ollama_base_url, )
# Warm up default model (prevents 30–90s cold-start on first request) from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage from app.llm import get_llm try: await get_llm().ainvoke([HumanMessage(content="ping")]) logger.info("Model pre-loaded: %s", settings.default_model) except Exception as e: logger.warning("Model warm-up failed (first request will be slow): %s", e)
# Build and store the compiled graph app.state.graph = build_graph() logger.info("Graph compiled and ready")
yield logger.info("Shutting down")
app = FastAPI( title="Multi-Agent System", version="1.0.0", lifespan=lifespan, )
app.add_middleware( CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"], # Vite dev server allow_credentials=True, allow_methods=["*"], allow_headers=["*"], )
app.include_router(v1_router, prefix="/api/v1")
@app.exception_handler(Exception) async def generic_error_handler(request, exc: Exception): logger.error("Unhandled exception: %s", str(exc)) return JSONResponse( status_code=500, content={"error": "Internal server error. Check server logs."}, )

api_routes_template.description: FastAPI route definitions. Routes call graph.ainvoke() with thread_id for session scoping.
api_routes_template.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.

config_template.description: Pydantic settings class. Customise fields to match implementation.md env table.
config_template.template:
# backend/app/config.py from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
class Settings(BaseSettings): # Ollama connection ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434" default_model: str = "llama3.2" llm_temperature: float = 0.7
# Per-agent model overrides (add one per agent from implementation.md) # supervisor_model: str = "phi4:mini" # research_model: str = "llama3.2" # classifier_model: str = "phi4:mini"
# Ollama performance — tune to your hardware ollama_num_ctx: int = 4096 # Context window tokens (Ollama default 2048 is too small) ollama_keep_alive: str = "5m" # How long to keep model in memory between calls
# API security api_key: str = "" # Empty string = no auth required
# Application log_level: str = "INFO" environment: str = "development"
model_config = SettingsConfigDict( env_file=".env", env_file_encoding="utf-8", case_sensitive=False, )
settings = Settings()

llm_factory_template.description: ChatOllama factory function. Lives in backend/app/llm.py. This is the ONLY place in the codebase that constructs ChatOllama. All graph nodes call get_llm() — never instantiate ChatOllama directly in nodes.
llm_factory_template.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers ChatOllama parameters — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
llm_factory_template.structural_pattern: File: backend/app/llm.py - Import: ChatOllama from langchain_ollama [verify exact import path via MCP] - Import: settings from app.config - Function signature: def get_llm(model: str | None = None, temperature: float | None = None) -> ChatOllama - Return: ChatOllama( model = model or settings.default_model, base_url = settings.ollama_base_url, temperature = temperature if temperature is not None else settings.llm_temperature, [CONTEXT_WINDOW_PARAM] = settings.ollama_num_ctx, [confirm param name via MCP] [KEEP_ALIVE_PARAM] = settings.ollama_keep_alive, [confirm param name via MCP] )

graph_state_template.description: State type definitions for the graph. MessagesState covers most cases. Extend only when routing metadata or custom fields are needed.
graph_state_template.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
graph_state_template.structural_pattern:
File: backend/app/graph/state.py
from [MCP-CONFIRMED-IMPORT] import MessagesState [verify import path via MCP]
For supervisor pattern — adds routing decision field: class SupervisorState(MessagesState): next_agent: str [set by supervisor_node, read by route_after_supervisor()]
For classifier/conditional routing: class RoutedState(MessagesState): route_label: str [set by classifier node, read by routing function]
Only define the state classes that implementation.md's graph pattern actually requires. Use plain MessagesState directly if no extra fields are needed.

schemas_template.description: Pydantic schemas for the API boundary. LangGraph handles internal state — only API models go here.
schemas_template.template:
# backend/app/models/schemas.py from typing import Any from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
# --- Request Models ---
class ChatRequest(BaseModel): session_id: str = Field(..., description="Unique session identifier — scopes conversation history") message: str = Field(..., min_length=1, description="User message") context: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
# --- Response Models ---
class ChatResponse(BaseModel): session_id: str content: str
# --- Task Models (for long-running async workflows) ---
class TaskResponse(BaseModel): task_id: str status: str # running
class TaskResult(BaseModel): task_id: str status: str # running | completed | failed result: str | None = None error: str | None = None

requirements_template.description: Python dependencies. LangGraph stack — add project-specific dependencies below the base set.
requirements_template.version_note: The version pins below were correct at time of writing but will age. BEFORE generating requirements.txt, check PyPI for latest stable versions: langchain-core: https://pypi.org/project/langchain-core/ langchain-ollama: https://pypi.org/project/langchain-ollama/ langgraph: https://pypi.org/project/langgraph/ Use >= version pins (not ==) so patch releases install automatically. Cross-reference with the versions confirmed by MCP queries during Phase 0 research.
requirements_template.template:
# backend/requirements.txt fastapi[standard]>=0.115.0 uvicorn[standard]>=0.30.0 pydantic>=2.0.0 pydantic-settings>=2.0.0 httpx>=0.27.0 python-dotenv>=1.0.0
# LangGraph + LangChain + Ollama langchain-core>=0.3.0 langchain-ollama>=0.2.0 langgraph>=0.2.0
# Testing pytest>=8.0.0 pytest-asyncio>=0.23.0
# Add project-specific dependencies below:

backend_dockerfile_template.description: Backend Dockerfile. Copies requirements.txt first for layer caching, then copies source. Runs as non-root user.
backend_dockerfile_template.template:
# backend/Dockerfile FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
# Install deps first (cached unless requirements.txt changes) COPY requirements.txt . RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000", "--reload"]

frontend_dockerfile_template.description: Frontend Dockerfile for development. Node 20 slim. Installs deps, runs Vite dev server bound to 0.0.0.0 so Docker port mapping works.
frontend_dockerfile_template.template:
# frontend/Dockerfile FROM node:20-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./ RUN npm install
COPY . .
EXPOSE 5173
CMD ["npm", "run", "dev", "--", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]

docker_compose_template.description: Root docker-compose.yml. Single command starts backend + frontend. Ollama runs on the host — containers reach it via host.docker.internal.
docker_compose_template.template:
# docker-compose.yml (project root) services: backend: build: ./backend ports: - "8000:8000" env_file: - ./backend/.env extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" # Linux: reach Ollama on host volumes: - ./backend:/app # Live reload — changes reflect without rebuild restart: unless-stopped
frontend: build: ./frontend ports: - "5173:5173" depends_on: - backend volumes: - ./frontend:/app # Live reload - /app/node_modules # Preserve container node_modules restart: unless-stopped
docker_compose_template.note: On macOS and Windows: host.docker.internal resolves automatically. On Linux: the extra_hosts entry maps it to the host gateway IP. OLLAMA_BASE_URL in backend/.env must be: http://host.docker.internal:11434

dockerignore_template.description: .dockerignore files — keeps images small, prevents secrets leaking into layers.
dockerignore_template.template: # backend/.dockerignore AND frontend/.dockerignore .env .env.* __pycache__/ *.pyc *.pyo .venv/ venv/ node_modules/ .git/ .gitignore *.md tests/ .pytest_cache/ .coverage dist/ build/

start_instructions_template.description: How to start the project. No shell scripts needed — Docker handles everything.
start_instructions_template.instructions:
# Starting the project (include in README.md):
## Prerequisites - Docker Desktop (or Docker Engine + Compose plugin) installed - Ollama installed on your host machine: https://ollama.ai/ - Required models pulled: ollama pull llama3.2 (and others from implementation.md)
## Start everything docker compose up --build
# Backend: http://localhost:8000 # API docs: http://localhost:8000/docs # Frontend: http://localhost:5173
## First time only — create backend .env cp backend/.env.example backend/.env # Edit backend/.env — set OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
## Stop everything docker compose down
## Rebuild after dependency changes docker compose up --build --force-recreate

env_example_template.description: .env.example — all variables from config.py with placeholder values.
env_example_template.template:
# backend/.env.example # Copy this file to .env and fill in your values
# Ollama connection # Local dev (outside Docker): http://localhost:11434 # Inside Docker containers: http://host.docker.internal:11434 OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434 DEFAULT_MODEL=llama3.2 LLM_TEMPERATURE=0.7
# Per-agent model overrides (uncomment and set to match your hardware tier) # SUPERVISOR_MODEL=phi4:mini # RESEARCH_MODEL=llama3.2 # CLASSIFIER_MODEL=phi4:mini
# Ollama performance (tune to your hardware — see 4_provider_ollama.xml hardware_requirements) OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=4096 # Token context window (Ollama default 2048 is too small for agents) OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=5m # How long to keep model loaded between requests (-1 = forever)
# API security (leave empty to disable authentication) API_KEY=
# Application LOG_LEVEL=INFO ENVIRONMENT=development

gitignore_template.description: Root .gitignore — prevents secrets and build artifacts from being committed.
gitignore_template.template:
# Secrets — NEVER commit these .env *.key *.pem
# Python __pycache__/ *.pyc *.pyo *.pyd .Python *.egg-info/ dist/ build/ .venv/ venv/
# Test / coverage .pytest_cache/ .coverage htmlcov/
# LangGraph / LangChain checkpoints.db
# OS .DS_Store Thumbs.db
# IDE .vscode/ .idea/ *.swp

conftest_template.description: Shared pytest fixtures. Mock LLM is injected into nodes via patch.
conftest_template.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
conftest_template.template:
# backend/tests/conftest.py import pytest from unittest.mock import AsyncMock from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage
@pytest.fixture def mock_llm(): """Mock ChatOllama that returns a configurable response. Patch app.graph.nodes.get_llm.""" llm = AsyncMock() llm.ainvoke.return_value = AIMessage(content="Mock response content") return llm

pyproject_template.description: pyproject.toml — required for pytest-asyncio to run async tests correctly.
pyproject_template.template: # backend/pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] # REQUIRED: without this, async test functions silently pass without running asyncio_mode = "auto" testpaths = ["tests"]
pyproject_template.note: Without asyncio_mode = "auto", async test functions are collected but never actually awaited — they appear to pass but do not run. This is a silent failure mode.

readme_template.description: Project README — generated once, then maintained manually.
readme_template.template:
# [Project Name] — Multi-Agent System
Brief description of what this system does.
## Prerequisites
- [Docker Desktop](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) (or Docker Engine + Compose plugin) - [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) installed on your host machine
## Setup
```bash # 1. Clone and enter the project git clone <repo-url> && cd <project-name>
# 2. Pull required Ollama models on your host ollama pull llama3.2 # (add one line per model from implementation.md)
# 3. Configure environment cp backend/.env.example backend/.env # Edit backend/.env — set OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434 ```
## Running
```bash docker compose up --build ```
Backend: http://localhost:8000 API docs: http://localhost:8000/docs Frontend: http://localhost:5173
## Environment Variables
| Variable | Default | Description | |----------|---------|-------------| | OLLAMA_BASE_URL | http://localhost:11434 | Ollama server URL | | DEFAULT_MODEL | llama3.2 | Default model for agents | | LLM_TEMPERATURE | 0.7 | Default LLM temperature | | OLLAMA_NUM_CTX | 4096 | Context window tokens | | OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE | 5m | Time to keep model loaded | | API_KEY | (empty) | Request auth key (empty = disabled) | | LOG_LEVEL | INFO | Logging verbosity |
## How to Add a New Agent Node
1. Add a tool (if needed): `backend/app/tools/your_tools.py` with `@tool` decorated functions 2. Add a node in `backend/app/graph/nodes.py`: - ReAct agent: `def create_your_agent(): return create_react_agent(get_llm(...), tools=[...])` - Custom node: `async def your_node(state: SupervisorState) -> dict:` 3. Register in `backend/app/graph/builder.py`: - `builder.add_node("your_agent", create_your_agent())` or `builder.add_node("your_node", your_node)` - Add it to `WORKER_AGENTS` list and the `add_conditional_edges` routing map 4. Add a model override to `config.py`: `your_agent_model: str = "llama3.2"` 5. Add `YOUR_AGENT_MODEL=llama3.2` to `.env.example`
## Running Tests
```bash docker compose exec backend pytest ```

production_readiness_checklist.description: Checklist to present to the user after all files are generated. Group into three tiers so the user knows what to do now vs. later.

tier.name: required_before_first_run
tier.item: Docker Desktop (or Docker Engine) installed and running
Ollama installed on host and required models pulled (ollama pull MODEL_NAME)
docker compose up --build completes without errors
Backend container starts: check docker compose logs backend

tier.name: recommended_before_sharing
tier.item: All generated tests pass (`pytest` from backend/)
`.env` is listed in `.gitignore` and NOT committed to git
API key set in `.env` if the API will be accessible to others
docker compose up --build from fresh clone works without manual steps
Tried a full conversation round-trip via POST /chat and got a valid response
Tested session persistence: two messages in the same session_id show correct context

tier.name: before_production_deployment
tier.item: MemorySaver replaced with SqliteSaver or PostgresSaver (see 7_best_practices.xml PH-1)
Running uvicorn with 1 worker only (MemorySaver is not multi-process safe)
Rate limiting configured for your expected request volume (PH-3)
Graceful shutdown timeout set to > max LLM timeout (PH-6)
Health check returns HTTP 503 when graph is not compiled (PH-7)
LangSmith tracing enabled (optional) for LLM call observability (PH-4)
CORS origins restricted to your actual frontend domain (not localhost)

generation_checklist.step: Generate config.py and models/schemas.py first (no dependencies)
Generate app/llm.py (get_llm factory — depends on config.py)
Generate app/tools/*.py (@tool functions from implementation.md Agent Inventory)
Generate app/graph/state.py, app/graph/nodes.py, app/graph/builder.py (depend on llm.py and tools/)
Generate api/v1/routes.py and main.py (depend on graph/builder.py)
Generate workflow files if implementation.md defines additional multi-step workflows
Generate backend/Dockerfile, frontend/Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, backend/.dockerignore, frontend/.dockerignore, backend/.env.example, .gitignore
Generate backend/requirements.txt, backend/pyproject.toml, backend/tests/conftest.py, backend/tests/test_graph.py stub
Present completion summary with manual steps checklist
